evidence: In indexer/hybrid, HybridConllUProcessor uses 'ConllU' suffix matching the codebase-wide pattern (BlackLabConllUIndexer, ConllUProcessor in tagging), but sibling SinglePassConlluProcessor in the same package uses 'Conllu' (lowercase 'u'), deviating from the established spelling.

tagging/ConllUProcessor and tagging/CorpusProcessor use SLF4J string concatenation (logger.info("Processing " + file)) while sibling CoNLL-U processors HybridConllUProcessor and SinglePassConlluProcessor use proper SLF4J placeholder style (logger.info("Processing {}", file)), creating a logging style island in the tagging package.
Package-level naming conventions (PascalCase classes, camelCase methods, UPPER_SNAKE constants) are applied uniformly across all 11 packages without drift.

Resource management protocol (implements Closeable) is applied consistently across hybrid indexer classes; minor Closeable vs AutoCloseable variation exists between packages but does not cause confusion.

strengths: Java naming conventions (PascalCase classes, camelCase methods, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE constants) are applied uniformly across all 11 packages with no cross-package drift.
Package boundaries are coherent: query predicates (AndPredicate, OrPredicate, LemmaPredicate, TagPredicate, WordPredicate, PosGroupPredicate) all implement TokenPredicate uniformly.
Resource management convention (implements Closeable) is applied consistently across the indexer/hybrid package for all stateful classes.
Builder pattern is used consistently in SentenceDocument.Builder with the same fluent-API style throughout.
convention_outlier.issue_character: Issues are localized to the CoNLL-U processor family: a single naming inconsistency within the indexer/hybrid package and a logging style island in the tagging package's sibling processors.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The codebase is largely coherent at the package and class-naming level, earning it above the 70-range. The main friction is the 'Conllu' vs 'ConllU' naming drift in sibling classes of the same package, and the string-concatenation logging style in the tagging package while all other logging-heavy classes use SLF4J placeholders. These are recurring but localized issues in one functional family, consistent with the 80 anchor of 'solid but uneven — a developer would mostly be well-served but would hit recurring friction when touching the CoNLL-U processing classes'.

issues.summary: SinglePassConlluProcessor uses 'Conllu' while all other CoNLL-U classes use 'ConllU'
evidence: SinglePassConlluProcessor.java line 33: 'public class SinglePassConlluProcessor implements Closeable' — 'Conllu' with lowercase 'u'.
HybridConllUProcessor.java line 29: 'public class HybridConllUProcessor implements Closeable' — 'ConllU' with uppercase 'U'.
ConllUProcessor.java (tagging) and BlackLabConllUIndexer.java also use 'ConllU' with uppercase 'U', making SinglePassConlluProcessor the sole outlier across all four CoNLL-U processing classes.
issues.suggestion: Rename SinglePassConlluProcessor to SinglePassConllUProcessor (capital 'U') to align with the codebase-wide 'ConllU' convention. Update the file name, class declaration, all internal references, and any callers.

issues.summary: tagging package uses SLF4J string concatenation while sibling CoNLL-U processors use placeholder syntax
evidence: tagging/ConllUProcessor.java line 59: logger.info("Processing CoNLL-U file: " + inputFile) — string concatenation.
tagging/CorpusProcessor.java line 40: logger.info("Starting corpus processing: " + inputFile) — string concatenation.
indexer/hybrid/HybridConllUProcessor.java line 60: logger.info("Processing CoNLL-U file: {}", inputFile) — SLF4J placeholder style.
indexer/LuceneIndexer.java uses SLF4J placeholder style throughout, making the tagging package a style island.
issues.suggestion: Convert all string-concatenation log calls in tagging/ConllUProcessor.java and tagging/CorpusProcessor.java to SLF4J placeholder syntax (e.g., logger.info("Processing CoNLL-U file: {}", inputFile)). This eliminates eager string construction on disabled log levels and aligns with the rest of the codebase.

root_causes: The CoNLL-U processor family grew incrementally (ConllUProcessor in tagging, then HybridConllUProcessor, then SinglePassConlluProcessor in indexer/hybrid), with each addition slightly diverging from the previous naming and logging conventions without a cross-class review.
